_description: Actual match outcomes and per-question RBP results from the JTC SportsPredict platform
actual_score: Australia 1-1 Egypt (Egypt won, presumably on the qualification tiebreak/penalties)

question_results.text: Will Mahmoud Trezeguet (Egypt) score a goal?
Will the match be tied at halftime?
Will the match have 2 or fewer total goals?
Will Nestory Irankunda (Australia) have 2 or more shots on target?
question_results.note: Wide-spread thin market correctly discounted based on rotational role.

question_results.text: Will both teams receive at least one card?
Will there be 4 or more total cards shown?
Will a penalty kick be awarded OR a red card be shown?
Will Egypt have 5 or more shots on target?
Will there be 9 or more total corner kicks?
Will Egypt score the first goal of the match?
Will the second half produce more goals than the first half?
Will there be 3 or more offside calls?
question_results.note: WORST RESULT OF MATCH. Priced low based on own-team-avg lambda; crowd's 53% was much closer. Same day COL-GHA priced this question type HIGH and also lost -- part of the 5-match offside pattern in JULY3_POSTMORTEM_DEEP_DIVE.md.

question_results.text: Will a substitution be made before halftime?
Will there be 20 or more total shots?
question_results.note: Biggest win of the match -- strong conviction on a DIRECT market question.

key_lessons: Biggest win: 20+ total shots (+29.46) -- DIRECT-tier market question, strong conviction paid off.

Biggest loss: 3+ offsides (-43.09) -- same-day mirror-image miss to COL-GHA's offside question (that one priced high and lost, this one priced low and lost). Core evidence behind the OFFSIDE_CROWD_ANCHOR proposed rule.
Irankunda 2+ SOT (+20.34) and Trezeguet scores (+7.66) both show real ESPN production data correctly discounting thin/wide-spread player markets.
